description:
You are the release-manager on a CI/CD box getting ready to hand off the next deployment artifacts.

Your job consists of exactly three (3) concrete steps that must be performed from the terminal:

1. Create a directory
   /home/user/deployment
   if it is not already present.

2. Inside that directory create a plain-text configuration file named
   /home/user/deployment/service_release.conf
   whose **exact** contents (including capitalisation, ordering, and the single trailing newline) are:

SERVICE_NAME=inventory-api
IMAGE_TAG=v1.4.0
REPLICAS=3
PORT=8080

3. In the same directory create a log file called
   /home/user/deployment/release_manifest.log
   containing one single line, **and nothing else**, with the text exactly:

service_release.conf:READY

That colon and the word READY must be written in uppercase and there must be a single final newline at the end of the line.

Summary of what the automated checker will look for:
• The directory /home/user/deployment must exist.  
• The file /home/user/deployment/service_release.conf must exist and match the four-line content shown above byte-for-byte.  
• The file /home/user/deployment/release_manifest.log must exist and contain exactly the single line  
  “service_release.conf:READY\n”  

Achieve this using any set of 3–4 reasonable shell commands. Your session should not require root privileges and everything must be created inside /home/user. When you are finished, the two files and their contents should satisfy the criteria so that an automated test script can verify them unambiguously.
truth:
Expected artefacts after the agent finishes:

1. Directory:
/home/user/deployment
   permissions: default user umask OK (not checked for exact value)

2. File:
/home/user/deployment/service_release.conf
   byte-for-byte content (including trailing newline):
SERVICE_NAME=inventory-api
IMAGE_TAG=v1.4.0
REPLICAS=3
PORT=8080\n

3. File:
/home/user/deployment/release_manifest.log
   byte-for-byte content (including trailing newline):
service_release.conf:READY\n
name: task_000000_02c76919
